figures.caption: Fig. 3: Experimental results. The top row illustrates the concentric-circles scenario, including the overlaid experiment video, the trajectory, and the velocity profile with motor inputs. The bottom row shows the separated-circles scenario, presenting the same set of components.
Fig. 5: Score vanishing and singularities in guiding vector field
Fig. 6: Path-following performance at polygon corners

Fig. 2: Visualization of the proposed SGVF framework on two waypoint-based path scenarios. The top row corresponds to the concentric-circles setup, showing the learned score field, tangent field, tangent network training loss, and the resulting path-following trajectories. The bottom row corresponds to the separated-circles setup, presenting the same components.
